reasoning:
FIDE rankings are updated monthly. The November 2025 ratings published on Wikipedia show Lei Tingjie at 2569. To achieve a 1% increase from this November 9 baseline, her rating would need to exceed 2594.69 (rounded to 2595).

1. On 2025-11-16, the FIDE rankings list will be identical to 2025-11-09 (the Nov 1 list), so reaching a 1% higher rating is virtually impossible (probability strictly capped by minimum bound of 0.05).
2. On 2025-12-09 (reflecting Dec 1 updates) and 2026-02-07 (reflecting Feb 1 updates), she would need an extraordinary 26 Elo jump. Considering her peak was around 2569 mid-to-late 2025, reaching 2595 would make her one of the highest-rated women in chess history. A +26 Elo gain at the 2570 level in standard time control over 1 to 3 months is extremely rare and there's no evidence of an incoming hot streak or volume of matches to support such a leap. Therefore, the probability remains low, increasing only marginally to reflect uncertainty over a slightly longer horizon.
